subheading: Turn one selfie into stylized profile pictures, relit portraits, and a talking avatar with open, editable ComfyUI workflows you control.
extendedDescription:
Most people who reach for an AI avatar generator have the same worry: will the result actually look like me, or just a vaguely similar stranger? That doubt is fair. The usual app makes you upload ten to twenty photos, trains a fresh model on your face, then invents new images from scratch, which is exactly why the output often drifts off your real features. The ComfyUI workflows on this page take a different route. They start from a single photo of you and transform it, so your identity stays anchored to a real image instead of a model's guess.

Three distinct jobs sit behind that promise. A profile-picture workflow turns one selfie into a grid of stylized variations, so you can pick the look you want for an avatar from photo without re-shooting anything. A character portrait relighting and skin-enhancement chain, built on Nano Banana Pro and Qwen-Image-Edit, cleans up a flat phone snapshot into a sharp, evenly lit portrait while keeping your face intact. And Kling Avatar 2.0 takes a still portrait plus an audio clip and produces a talking avatar with lip sync, which is how you get an animated avatar speaking your words without ever opening a camera.

Knowing which tool does what saves you time. If you want a fun, varied profile picture maker result, the stylized grid is the fastest path: one upload, several styles, no training step. If you need a clean, believable headshot-style portrait, run the relighting and skin enhancer in sequence and stop when it looks right. If you want a character avatar that moves and speaks, feed a finished still into Kling Avatar 2.0 with your audio and it animates the mouth to match.

Be clear about what these workflows do not do. They will not learn a brand-new pose library of you or place you in scenes you were never photographed in, because none of them trains a custom model on a photo set. What you get is a faithful edit or animation of a real image, which is usually what people actually want when they say custom avatar: their own face, looking sharp, in the style or motion they choose.

Because every step is open, you decide the style, the lighting direction, how far to push detail, and which audio drives the talking avatar. There is no watermark and no hidden model quietly reshaping your face. You can run the whole thing locally in ComfyUI or on Comfy Cloud with nothing to install, and re-run any pass until the avatar matches how you actually look. Start with a clear, front-facing photo, pick the job that fits your goal, and iterate as many times as you like until it matches.

styles.title: Stylized profile pictures
styles.description: Turn one selfie into a grid of stylized profile-picture variations, then pick the avatar you like best.

styles.title: Lighting migration
styles.description: Borrow the lighting from a reference image you like and apply it to your portrait for a consistent mood.

styles.title: Talking avatar with Kling
styles.description: Feed a still portrait plus audio into Kling Avatar 2.0 to get an animated avatar that speaks with lip sync.

howToUse: Decide your goal: a stylized profile picture, a clean relit portrait, or a talking avatar.
Open the matching workflow on Comfy Cloud with no install, or load it in your local ComfyUI.
Upload one clear, front-facing photo with your face unobstructed and evenly visible.
For stills, set the style or lighting and how strongly to enhance skin, starting conservative.
For a talking avatar, attach an audio clip so Kling Avatar 2.0 can drive the lip sync.
Run the workflow, compare against your original, then download the version that looks most like you.

faqItems.question: How does an AI avatar generator work in ComfyUI?
faqItems.answer: Instead of training a model on a folder of your photos, these workflows take one image you already have and transform it. Some produce stylized profile pictures, others relight and enhance a portrait, and Kling Avatar 2.0 animates a still into a talking avatar. Each step is exposed so you can adjust it.

faqItems.question: Can I make an avatar from a photo without uploading many images?
faqItems.answer: Yes. Every workflow here starts from a single front-facing photo, so there is no photo set to gather or model to train. Because the result edits or animates your real image, the avatar from photo stays faithful to your actual features.

faqItems.question: Will the avatar actually look like me?
faqItems.answer: These workflows transform your real photo rather than inventing a new face, so your features stay intact. You also control how far the style and skin enhancement push, which keeps the output recognizable. If a pass starts to drift, lower the strength and run it again until it matches how you look.

faqItems.question: Can I make a talking avatar without a camera or recording myself on video?
faqItems.answer: Yes. Kling Avatar 2.0 takes a still portrait and an audio clip, then animates the mouth to match the speech, giving you an animated avatar that talks. You never need to film yourself, only supply one photo and the audio you want it to say.

faqItems.question: Can I run these AI avatar generator workflows myself?
faqItems.answer: Yes. The workflows are open and editable. You can run them locally in ComfyUI, or on Comfy Cloud with nothing to install, and there are no watermarks on the output.

faqItems.question: What avatar styles can I create from one selfie?
faqItems.answer: The profile picture maker workflow produces a grid of stylized variations from a single selfie, so you can compare several looks at once. You can also relight the portrait for a clean, studio mood or push it toward a more polished, photographic finish.

faqItems.question: Can I keep the same character avatar across several images?
faqItems.answer: Because each result starts from the same source photo, your face stays consistent from one render to the next. These workflows preserve identity rather than reinventing it, so a custom avatar you make once reads as the same person across the variations you generate.

faqItems.question: Do these workflows train a custom model on my face like other apps?
faqItems.answer: No, and that is intentional. None of these workflows learns a model from a photo set, so they will not place you in poses you were never photographed in. They edit or animate a real image instead, which keeps the avatar honest to the photo you started from.

faqItems.question: Do I need to install anything, or can I run this in the browser?
faqItems.answer: You do not have to install anything. Comfy Cloud opens the same workflows in your browser and runs them on remote GPUs, so there is no setup and no Docker. If you prefer to keep everything on your own machine, the identical graphs load in local ComfyUI.

faqItems.question: What hardware do I need to run the portrait edits locally?
faqItems.answer: The Nano Banana Pro relighting and Kling Avatar 2.0 steps call cloud API nodes, so they run on a modest machine since the heavy work happens remotely. The local Qwen-Image-Edit skin pass needs roughly 16GB of VRAM in its FP8 build, and the GGUF quantizations scale down to around 8GB if your GPU is smaller. Comfy Cloud is a paid plan if you would rather skip local hardware entirely.
